dict.string:
PowerUtil has been built with ❤️ by iTech Everything.
You can support the creator on the following platforms: youtube.com/TheiTE twitter.com/TheOriginaliTE
For more information and other shortcuts like this, visit itecheverything.com/shortcuts
Incorrect file. Choose the ￼ file, from Settings.
This shortcut is made for iOS users only.
This shortcut is made for iOS users only.

Please click "Analytics Data" on your screen. -> Click or search for any one the ￼ file(s). -> Click Share on the top right -> Click PowerUtil
If you have "Share ￼ Analytics" disabled or have the ￼ file missing, you will need to enable them and wait for at least 24 hours.
You can choose to set a reminder for later, make sure you've enabled "Share ￼ Analytics" after setting the reminder.

Please go to Privacy -> Analytics & Improvements and click "Analytics Data" on your screen. -> Click or search for any one the ￼ file(s). -> Click Share on the top right -> Click PowerUtil
If you have "Share ￼ Analytics" disabled or have the ￼ file missing, you will need to enable them and wait for at least 24 hours.
You can choose to set a reminder for later, make sure you've enabled "Share ￼ Analytics" after setting the reminder.

PowerUtil Calculated Battery Health

￼%

This is a measure of your current battery capacity compared to the battery capacity (in mAh) that Apple lists on their website.

Maximum Battery Capacity (Battery Health)

￼%

This is a measure of battery capacity relative to when it was new. Lower capacity may result in fewer hours of usage between charges.

Note: Apple did not provide any Battery Health details in this week's analytics. PowerUtil has calculated it for you. To see the battery health calculated by Apple, please wait for upto a week, and then try running this shortcut again to see if the battery analytics have been made available. If you see multiple ￼ files in Settings, try running the shortcut with each one of them. If the issue still persists, do not worry, it is common for Apple to not include Battery Stats every now and then, unfortunately, we can't do anything but wait.

Note: Apple did not provide any Battery details in this week's analytics, neither was PowerUtil able to calculate it. To fix this, please wait for upto a week, and then try running this shortcut again to see if the battery analytics have been made available. If you see multiple ￼ files in Settings, try running the shortcut with each one of them. If the issue still persists, do not worry, it is common for Apple to not include Battery Stats every now and then, unfortunately, we can't do anything but wait.

Note: It looks like you are using one of the newer devices. Apple has stopped providing the raw values for the battery data, which are required for calculating 'PowerUtil Calculated Battery Health', and your device therefore cannot show it. Please refer to the 'Battery Health' section instead, which is provided straight from Apple.

PowerUtil

Cycle Count

￼ cycles

This is a measure of how many times your ￼'s battery has been drained from 100 to 0. This need not happen at one go

For example, if your ￼ goes from 100 to 50, you recharge it, and it falls back to 50 once again, this counts as one cycle.

Average Temperature

￼°C

￼ ￼ ￼
